statement.markdown: Implement a bubble sort algorithm that sorts an array of integers in ascending order. The bubble sort algorithm repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements, and swaps them if they are in the wrong order. The pass through the list is repeated until the list is sorted.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a list of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a sorted list of integers in ascending order.
statement.notes: The implementation should be efficient and not contain any syntax errors.

constraints.description: The number of elements in the input array.

examples.explanation: The input array is sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: The bubble sort algorithm is not efficient for large inputs.
The implementation should handle edge cases correctly.

editorial.title: Bubble Sort Algorithm
editorial.intuition: The bubble sort algorithm is a simple sorting algorithm that works by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to use a nested loop to compare and swap adjacent elements.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient approach is to use a flag to track if any swaps were made in a pass.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a flag to track if any swaps were made in a pass and to break the loop if no swaps were made.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the algorithm repeatedly compares and swaps adjacent elements, resulting in a sorted list.
editorial.dryRun: The dry run shows that the algorithm works by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements.
commonMistakes: Not using a flag to track if any swaps were made.
Not breaking the loop if no swaps were made.
